title: BUbuisnessAndMarket
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbuisnessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Goldman Sachs vice chair on the hidden trap of senior management: ‘pretty soon the bosses are no longer watching you’
items.description: Before returning as vice chair, Rob Kaplan served on the faculty of the Harvard Business School and as president of the Dallas Federal Reserve.

items.title: Asia’s founders are spending more money on AI tools, with use of some coding tools rising by more than four times
items.description: Spending on AI tools by founders rose 20% last year, according to Aspire, a Singapore-based fintech firm.

items.title: Want to live forever? Meta patented an AI model that would keep your profile active after you die
items.description: Meta’s new AI patent means your dead uncle could still comment fire emojis on your posts, but experts say this could undermine grief and closure.

items.title: JPMorgan’s CEO Jamie Dimon reveals the career goal he adopted when he was just a 28-year-old assistant: Do not speak unless you can add value
items.description: Before Jamie Dimon was the billionaire boss of JPMorgan, he set himself a surprisingly modest rule: don’t talk—it propelled him to a CFO role by 30

items.title: Boards aren’t ready for the AI age: What happens when your CEO gets deepfaked?
